skill.name: bullmq-specialist
skill.description: Expert guidance for BullMQ queues, scheduled jobs, worker patterns, Redis-backed reliability, and production background processing in Node.js and TypeScript.
skill.summary: Design, debug, and improve BullMQ job queues for reliable Node.js background processing.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found no executable shell usage or weak cryptographic logic. The backtick alerts at SKILL.md:57 are Markdown formatting around related skill names. The weak cryptography alerts at SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:14, and SKILL.md:16 are substring false positives in descriptive text.

content.value_statement: Background jobs can fail silently, overload Redis, or block user workflows. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design reliable BullMQ queues, workers, schedules, and recovery patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains production-ready BullMQ queue setup and configuration choices.
Advises on delayed jobs, scheduled jobs, repeatable jobs, and priorities.
Guides worker concurrency, rate limiting, event handling, and retry behavior.
Designs job flows with dependencies and parent-child processing.
Identifies common queue anti-patterns such as giant payloads and missing dead letter handling.
limitations: Does not connect to Redis or inspect live queues by itself.
Does not include runnable scripts, tests, or reference implementation files.
Requires project context to tailor advice to a specific BullMQ version.
Provides guidance only; operators must validate changes in staging.

use_cases.title: Plan a queue architecture
use_cases.description: Design queues, workers, retries, and Redis usage for a new background processing feature.

use_cases.title: Improve job reliability
use_cases.description: Review stuck jobs, retry behavior, rate limits, and dead letter handling before production incidents grow.

use_cases.title: Model multi-step workflows
use_cases.description: Map parent-child job flows for emails, AI tasks, imports, exports, and other long-running work.

prompt_templates.title: Review a basic queue
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my BullMQ queue and worker setup. Explain missing reliability settings, retry choices, and Redis configuration concerns in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Design scheduled jobs
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a BullMQ approach for delayed and repeatable jobs. Include scheduling strategy, deduplication, retries, and operational checks.

prompt_templates.title: Tune worker throughput
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my worker concurrency, rate limiting, and job priorities. Recommend changes that improve throughput without overloading Redis or downstream services.

prompt_templates.title: Architect job flows
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a BullMQ flow for a multi-step process with dependencies. Include failure handling, idempotency, observability, and recovery procedures.

output_examples.input: A team needs to process uploads, generate thumbnails, and send notifications after success.
output_examples.output: The response outlines separate queues for uploads, image work, and notifications. It recommends dependency-aware flow design, retries, idempotency, and dead letter handling.

output_examples.input: A worker falls behind during traffic spikes and Redis CPU rises sharply.
output_examples.output: The response prioritizes measuring job duration, reducing payload size, tuning concurrency, adding rate limits, and checking Redis capacity before scaling workers.

output_examples.input: A nightly report job sometimes runs twice and sends duplicate emails.
output_examples.output: The response recommends deterministic job identifiers, idempotent report generation, lock awareness, audit logging, and safe retry behavior.

best_practices: Keep job payloads small and store large data in durable application storage.
Use idempotent processors, bounded retries, and dead letter handling for failure recovery.
Monitor Redis, queue depth, worker errors, and job latency before changing concurrency.
anti_patterns: Do not put large documents or binary data directly into job payloads.
Do not run workers with unbounded concurrency against shared Redis infrastructure.
Do not rely on retries without idempotency, observability, and dead letter handling.

faq.question: Does this skill execute BullMQ commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for queue design and review. It does not run commands or connect to Redis.

faq.question: Can it help with TypeScript projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is intended for Node.js and TypeScript applications that use BullMQ.

faq.question: Can it diagnose stuck jobs?
faq.answer: It can guide diagnosis using queue state, Redis health, worker logs, retries, and processor behavior.

faq.question: Does it cover repeatable jobs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes delayed jobs, scheduled jobs, repeatable jobs, and priority handling.

faq.question: Is it only for new projects?
faq.answer: No. It also helps review existing queues, improve reliability, and plan safer production changes.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide queue names, job types, retry rules, worker counts, Redis constraints, and observed failure symptoms.
